context: Tests whether the agent uses the correct python-pptx patterns for .pptx generation: the specific undocumented template-stripping internals (_sldIdLst + drop_rel), a separate batch speaker-notes injection step after slides are created, and footer on every slide.

checklist.name: Uses _sldIdLst
checklist.description: The script accesses `slides._sldIdLst` (the undocumented internal attribute) to iterate over slide IDs during template stripping — not a public API like slides[0] or clear()

checklist.name: Uses drop_rel()
checklist.description: The script calls `part.drop_rel(rId)` or `tmpl.part.drop_rel(rId)` to remove the relationship during template stripping — not a delete or pop operation

checklist.name: Correct relationship namespace URI
checklist.description: The relationship ID is retrieved using the exact namespace URI string: '{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships}id' — not a shortened version or variable substitution

checklist.name: Template stripping before content
checklist.description: The template stripping code (removing existing slides) appears BEFORE any new slide additions — the script does not add content slides to a template that still has demo slides

checklist.name: Speaker notes in separate step
checklist.description: Speaker notes are injected in a SEPARATE code block, function, or loop that runs AFTER all slides have been created — not inline during each slide's creation (e.g., a dedicated notes injection pass over existing slides)

checklist.name: notes_text_frame access pattern
checklist.description: Speaker notes are set via `slide.notes_slide.notes_text_frame.text = ...` (or equivalent attribute access chain) — not via placeholder manipulation or XML editing

checklist.name: Footer on slides
checklist.description: The script adds footer text to slides (e.g., via manage_text, add_textbox, or a helper function) — not absent or only mentioned in comments

checklist.name: Final save call
checklist.description: The script includes a save() call (tmpl.save(...) or prs.save(...)) to persist the generated .pptx to disk

checklist.name: Background color applied
checklist.description: The script sets a background color for slides (not leaving all slides at the default white), referencing the background_color_strategy or using a color value that is NOT #FFFFFF / white
